name: Hideki Tojo
title: Prime Minister and Minister of War

red_lines: American oil embargo and economic strangulation
Loss of conquered territories in Greater East Asia
core_narratives: Greater East Asia Co-Prosperity Sphere liberating Asia from Western colonialism
Bushido warrior spirit as national destiny
Japan's divine mission under the Emperor

decision_history.event_description: US oil embargo and Hull Note threatening Japanese strategic position
decision_history.decision_taken: Recommended war against United States, Britain, and Netherlands to Cabinet and Emperor; authorized Pearl Harbor attack
decision_history.outcome: Spectacular initial victories across Pacific but awakened American industrial might
context_factors: gamble on American willingness to negotiate after losses

decision_history.event_description: Japanese forces achieved rapid conquest across Southeast Asia and Pacific
decision_history.decision_taken: Ordered expansion to maximum defensive perimeter including Burma, Dutch East Indies, and Pacific islands
decision_history.outcome: Secured oil and rubber resources but created overextended defensive perimeter
context_factors: Dutch East Indies oil fields captured
desire to establish fait accompli before US mobilization

decision_history.event_description: Battle of Midway resulted in loss of four aircraft carriers
decision_history.decision_taken: Suppressed news of defeat domestically; shifted to defensive strategy while maintaining offensive rhetoric
decision_history.outcome: Strategic initiative permanently lost; began attritional island defense

information_sources: Army and Navy chiefs of staff
cultural_context.domestic_power_base: Imperial Japanese Army (Control Faction), Emperor's confidence, Kempeitai military police, zaibatsu industrial combines

military_posture.force_disposition: navy-centric Pacific defense with army committed in China; resource logistics increasingly strained
